dataset.name: India Retail Fashion — 2026 Calendar Signals
dataset.scope: Verifiable public calendar events only (Tier A). No editorial trend claims from third-party publishers, no numerical sales-uplift forecasts.

methodology.principle: Every signal is either (a) a fixed calendar date, (b) a lunar/Hijri date computed from a Panchang, or (c) an event date announced by the rights-holder. No trend name is taken from a fashion publication without direct source verification. No numerical sales impact is claimed — that must come from the retailer's own historical sales data.

dateConfidenceLevels.HIGH: Fixed Gregorian date, cannot shift (national holidays, Christian liturgical dates that are widely published a year in advance).
dateConfidenceLevels.MEDIUM_ANNOUNCED: Announced by rights-holder (movie studio, sports body) but can shift. Verify a week before displaying to end users.
dateConfidenceLevels.LUNAR_VERIFY: Computed from Hindu Panchang or Islamic Hijri calendar. Regional almanacs or moon-sighting can shift by 1 day. Verify with the retailer's regional almanac before customer-facing use.

methodology.planningReviewGuidance: planningReviewStart is set ~90 days before the event for most fashion buying lead times. Adjust to the retailer's actual OTB lead time (typically 60–120 days).
methodology.important: The 'planningRelevance' block on each signal is analytical guidance for a merchandiser, not a source-published prediction. Direction words ('WATCH', 'REVIEW', 'TEST') are conservative — they say 'look at your historical data for this window,' not 'this will happen.'

planningRelevance.reasoning: Public holiday triggers year-end/New-Year party demand. Review last year's Dec 25–Jan 5 window sales before setting allocation.
Solar-calendar harvest festival with strong regional apparel tradition (yellow tones in South, traditional weaves in Gujarat). Use retailer's prior-year regional sales.
Long-standing national retail promotion window. Buyer should align markdown / promo depth calendar. Note the tricolour graphic-tee category is typically active in this window.

sourceCitation.name: Hindu Panchang — verify with regional almanac
sourceCitation.note: Confirm date with retailer's regional Panchang before publishing

planningRelevance.reasoning: Cultural festival with yellow-colour tradition, strongest in East and North India. Cross-check retailer's historical yellow-tone sell-through around this window.

sourceCitation.name: Hindu Panchang — verify with regional almanac
sourceCitation.note: Confirm date with retailer's regional Panchang before publishing

planningRelevance.reasoning: Major devotional festival, secondary apparel driver. Review category historical.

sourceCitation.name: Hindu Panchang — Phalguna Purnima 2026
sourceCitation.note: Widely reported as March 4, 2026. Confirm with retailer's regional Panchang.

planningRelevance.reasoning: Colour-play festival. Historical demand pattern: white bases and easy-care cotton pre-Holi; replacement casual wear post-Holi. Review both windows in retailer's prior-year data.

sourceCitation.name: Islamic Hijri calendar — moon-sighting confirmation required
sourceCitation.note: Widely calculated as March 21, 2026, but actual date depends on Shawwal crescent moon sighting. Can shift by 1 day.

planningRelevance.reasoning: Major Muslim festival with strong new-clothes tradition. Kurta / suit / kids ethnic categories peak. Review retailer's prior-year window.

sourceCitation.name: Hindu Panchang — verify with regional almanac

sourceCitation.name: IPL Official Schedule (BCCI)
sourceCitation.note: Confirm 2026 exact tournament dates from official schedule. Window shown is typical late-Mar to end-May pattern.

planningRelevance.reasoning: Sustained-attention sports window. Sports-inspired casual wear historically active. IMPORTANT: team names, franchise marks and league logos are trademarked — do not use without a licensing agreement.
Solemn observance day. Retailer promo depth typically light. Review historical for Kerala / Goa / North-East markets.
Christian celebration; new-clothes and kids party-wear tradition in strong-Christian markets.
Public holiday, mid-week off. Modest promo window; review historical footfall.

sourceCitation.note: Verify with regional almanac

sourceCitation.name: Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures release calendar

IMPORTANT: FIFA, tournament, and national-team marks are trademarked — do not use without licence. Generic sports-inspired casual is safe.

sources.name: Government of India — Public Holidays 2026
sources.notes: Authoritative list of central-government-recognised holidays. State/optional holidays vary.

sources.name: Hindu Panchang (Regional)
sources.notes: Hindu festival dates are computed from lunar Panchang. Regional almanacs can differ by 1 day. No single URL is authoritative; verify with the retailer's regional almanac (e.g., North Indian, Bengali, Malayalam, Tamil versions).

sources.name: Islamic Hijri Calendar
sources.notes: Muslim festival dates depend on moon-sighting and can shift by 1 day. Confirm locally near event.

sources.name: IPL Official Schedule (BCCI)
sources.notes: Team names, franchise marks and league logos are trademarked. Referencing the tournament window is fine; using marks is not without a licence.

sources.name: FIFA World Cup 2026
sources.notes: FIFA and national-team marks are trademarked. Referencing the tournament window is fine; using marks is not without a licence.

sources.name: Marvel Studios Movie Calendar
sources.notes: Release dates are public. Character names, film titles, poster artwork and logos are Marvel/Disney-copyrighted — do not reproduce.

sources.name: Walt Disney Studios Release Calendar
sources.notes: Release dates are public. All Disney character names, film artwork and logos are copyrighted — do not reproduce.

sources.name: Christian Liturgical Calendar 2026
sources.notes: Easter 2026 = April 5. Good Friday and other dates derived from that anchor.

sources.name: State Education Department Academic Calendars 2026
sources.notes: School break windows vary by state and board. The window in this dataset is the widest common range.

safeToDisplay: The 'planningRelevance' block, labelled clearly as analyst guidance
The source citation (name + URL if provided)
requiresLicensingOrLegalReview: Character graphics, movie posters, film logos (Marvel, Disney, etc.)
Team names, franchise logos, tournament marks (IPL, FIFA, national teams)
Screenshots, quoted text from third-party fashion publications (Vogue, Pinterest, etc.)
Third-party pageviews / trend charts (Google Trends, YouTube stats) when re-hosted
neverDisplay: A numerical sales-uplift percentage attributed to an event, unless it comes from the retailer's own historical sales data for that event window
A confidence percentage attributed to a third-party publisher (they do not publish confidence — it is an analyst-assigned number)
Presenting a lunar-calculated date as a fixed fact without the 'verify locally' note
